summary: Replace console logging with environment-gated logger
problem_statement: The codebase uses raw console.log and console.error throughout production code paths. Debug logging reaches end users in production, clutters browser consoles, and can leak sensitive information (request bodies, internal state). A lightweight logger module gated by NODE_ENV centralises control over what is logged and when.
in_scope: Create a lightweight logger module with log levels (debug, info, warn, error) gated by NODE_ENV
Replace console.log and console.error calls in production code paths with the logger
Ensure debug-level logging is suppressed in production (NODE_ENV=production)
Ensure the logger is usable on both server and client sides
out_of_scope: Integrating with external logging services (Winston transports, Datadog, etc.)
Structured logging (JSON log lines) — a simple console wrapper is sufficient for R2
Changing request logging middleware — handled in P6-R2-21
Log rotation, file-based logging, or log aggregation
requirements: A logger module exists with environment-gated log levels
Debug-level output is suppressed when NODE_ENV=production
All raw console.log/console.error calls in production code paths are replaced with logger calls
The logger works in both server (Node) and client (browser) environments
acceptance_criteria: Production debug logging is removed or routed through the logger module
The logger suppresses debug output in production
No raw console.log/console.error calls remain in refactored production code paths
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
dependencies: None — the logger is a standalone utility
risks: Some console.log calls may be intentional operational output (server startup messages) — these should use info level, not be removed
implementation_notes: A minimal implementation: export functions (debug, info, warn, error) that check a LOG_LEVEL or NODE_ENV before calling the underlying console method. No external dependencies needed. Place in a shared lib/logger or utils/logger module.
